title: As many say software is dead, Amazon Web Services CEO Matt Garman calls it a 'huge ...'
content: AWS CEO Matt Garman views the AI-driven 'SaaSpocalypse' as a major business opportunity, not a threat.  He highlighted the rise of agentic AI reshaping work and announced new AI-powered tools like the Quick desktop app, aiming to revolutionize personal productivity.  AWS is also deepening its OpenAI partnership, integrating advanced AI models into its cloud services to meet surging demand.

title: Mamata Banerjee vs Suvendu Adhikari: All eyes on high-stakes battle in Bhowanipore
content: West Bengal's high-stakes political battle intensifies as Mamata Banerjee and Suvendu Adhikari face off in Bhabanipur.  While Banerjee has a strong history in the constituency, a significant number of Muslim voters are under scrutiny, potentially impacting the TMC's victory margin.

title: ‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: As Accenture rolls out Microsoft Copilot to staff, CEO says teams doing higher-value work
content: Microsoft's Copilot 365 AI assistant is now powering Accenture's workforce of 743,000 employees, marking the largest enterprise deal for the chatbot. This significant adoption by Accenture, a leading AI adopter, highlights Microsoft's strategy to boost paid Copilot subscriptions. Early results show employees completing routine tasks up to 15 times faster, with major productivity gains reported.

title: Rape-murder of 3-year-old: Kin seek death, protest shuts down Pune district
content: A brutal rape and murder of a three-year-old girl has sparked outrage in Pune's Bhor-Rajgad belt, leading to a shutdown. The victim's family refuses to meet politicians until the 65-year-old accused, a repeat offender, is sentenced to death. Protests erupted, with the Chief Minister assuring a fast-track trial and pursuit of the death penalty, while cautioning against public execution demands.

title: IPL: Sai Sudharsan scripts history, shatters Chris Gayle’s 13-year record
content: Gujarat Titans opener Sai Sudharsan has rewritten IPL history, surpassing Chris Gayle for the most runs after 50 matches with 2178 runs. He also recently became the fastest to 2000 runs in just 47 innings. Sudharsan's consistent performances, including a century and three fifties in his last four innings, highlight his remarkable rise.

title: Tensions intensify at Strait: Cargo ship sailing near Hormuz attacked by Iran
content: A bulk carrier was attacked by small craft near Iran's coast in the Strait of Hormuz, with all crew reported safe and no environmental impact. This incident occurs amidst heightened tensions as the US enforces a counter-blockade on Iranian ports and plans to guide ships through the strait, drawing a strong warning from Tehran.

title: 8 dead, several injured after car rams into group aiding accident victims in Uttar Pradesh
content: A speeding car tragically killed eight people and injured several others on the Akbarpur–Jalalpur road. The car rammed into a group of rescuers who had gathered to help victims of an earlier motorcycle collision. Police are investigating the incident and attempting to identify the car's driver.

title: Shivang Kumar: How father transformed the swashbuckling opener into a chinaman in a year!
content: Shivang Kumar made his name in the Madhya Pradesh T20 League as an opener. His exploits with the bat helped him become a marquee player for Bundelkhand Bulls, who picked him for Rs 13 lakh for the 2026 season. But within a year, he learnt the art of wrist-spin from his father, a former Bengal U-19 cricketer, and made instant impact. The IPL franchises noticed and multiple trials later, he was roped in by the Sunrisers Hyderabad.

title: Middle East crisis: Iran proposes 30-day plan to end war, says it has received US reply
content: Iran has received a US response to its 30-day peace proposal, conveyed via Pakistan. The offer aims to end the ongoing war, not just extend a ceasefire, and suggests delaying nuclear talks until after hostilities cease. The US President expressed doubt about the proposal's success, citing Iran's past actions.

title: More Strait talks: Trump rolls out 'Project Freedom' to guide ships through Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump launched 'Project Freedom' to aid commercial ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, citing humanitarian concerns and low supplies.  He stated the US will guide vessels safely out, emphasizing these ships belong to nations uninvolved in regional disputes. Trump also revealed positive discussions with Iran, though he rejected their latest proposal for conflict resolution.

title: PSL final: Mohsin Naqvi hands trophy as Babar Azam lifts first T20 title as captain
content: Peshawar Zalmi secured their second PSL title, their first since 2017, by defeating Hyderabad Kingsmen in the final. Captain Babar Azam finally lifted a trophy as leader, while Aaron Hardie starred with both bat and ball, scoring an unbeaten 56 and taking 4 wickets to guide Zalmi to victory.

title: Will Mumbai Indians allow Jasprit Bumrah the rest he badly needs?
content: Jasprit Bumrah is shouldering a heavy workload for Mumbai Indians in the IPL, struggling to deliver wickets while bowling defensively and attacking. Despite MI's dismal performance and Bumrah's fatigue, coach Mahela Jayawardene is reluctant to rest him, citing mathematical possibilities. This approach echoes past decisions that led to Bumrah's injury and significant absences for India.

title: Today’s poll results may give a broad mood of nation; how it may impact equations nationally
content: Assembly poll results in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry will offer a glimpse into the national political mood. The outcomes could significantly impact the opposition INDIA bloc, with key contests in Bengal and Tamil Nadu holding particular importance for regional giants DMK and TMC.

title: Are Huma Qureshi and Rachit Singh set to get married in October this year? - Report
content: Huma Qureshi and Rachit Singh are reportedly planning to tie the knot in October or November of 2026. Following a private engagement in the US, the couple is said to be organizing an intimate wedding ceremony, likely in Mumbai, with a grand reception for industry friends to follow. Their relationship gained attention after they were seen together at various events. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Abandoned in forest, 2-year-old walks for 20 hours, survives
content: A two-year-old boy miraculously survived nearly 20 hours alone in a wild animal-infested forest after his father allegedly murdered his mother and abandoned him. Found weak and dehydrated by police, the toddler's incredible resilience in the face of extreme danger has stunned authorities. The father has been arrested, and the child is recovering.

title: Air India CEO race narrows to 4 names
content: The race for Air India's CEO position has narrowed to four candidates, including two Indian executives, Nipun Aggarwal and Vinod Kannan, and two expatriates. The airline's board is set to meet soon to make a decision amidst significant financial losses and operational challenges. The delay in appointing a successor to Campbell Wilson is reportedly adding to the airline's ongoing struggles.

title: Heart donated in Panchkula gives Sudanese teen in Delhi new life
content: A 14-year-old Sudanese boy received a life-saving heart transplant in Delhi, thanks to a donor heart flown from Haryana. The organ came from a 41-year-old woman declared brain dead, whose family bravely consented to donation.

title: From red to green to saffron? BJP eyes power shift in Bengal as Mamata defends her turf
content: West Bengal's political landscape, historically dominated by single parties for extended periods, is now witnessing an intense battle for the 2026 assembly elections. Chief minister Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule faces a formidable challenge from the aggressively expanding BJP, which aims to end the TMC's reign. This election marks a critical juncture for the state's political legacy.

title: SG Tushar Mehta takes swipe at ‘bully’ judges in his book
content: Senior advocate Tushar Mehta's upcoming book criticizes some judges for exhibiting 'judicial bullying' due to excessive courtroom reverence. He argues that while judges face immense pressure, this cannot justify incivility, as the public expects high standards from an institution they cannot easily remove. Mehta highlights the power imbalance lawyers face when confronting arrogant judges.

title: Team Chadha to get audience with Murmu ahead of Mann
content: Punjab's political landscape is heating up as rival factions, led by AAP MP Raghav Chadha and CM Bhagwant Mann, are set to meet the President. The dispute centers on alleged defections and police actions, with Chadha planning to highlight a "political vendetta" following FIRs against fellow MP Sandeep Pathak. BJP has also voiced concerns over alleged retaliation.

title: Canadian intelligence agency accuses India of interference, spying
content: Canada's intelligence agency has listed India and China among nations involved in foreign interference. While acknowledging historical covert actions by India, the report highlights Khalistan extremism as a significant national security threat to Canada. This comes amid efforts to mend ties between Ottawa and New Delhi, with the latest report notably downplaying previous accusations against India.

title: Bajaj Finance, Waaree Engineering & more: Top stocks to watch today
content: Analysts are bullish on Bajaj Finance, IIFL Finance, and Navin Fluorine, citing strong quarterly results and positive future outlooks. Federal Bank is seen as fairly valued, while Waaree Engineering's revenue beat expectations despite margin concerns. Investors are watching these key companies closely.

title: ‘My bank account has been frozen’: Shakib reveals condition for B'desh return
content: Shakib Al Hasan said he is ready to return to Bangladesh and face ongoing legal cases if assured “normal security” and protection from harassment. The former captain, absent from Bangladesh since May 2024, also revealed his bank account has been frozen during investigations. Shakib denied wrongdoing in multiple cases and said authorities have found no evidence against him so far.

title: India dismisses Nepal’s Kailash yatra objection over Lipulekh Pass
content: Nepal has officially objected to India resuming the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra through Lipulekh Pass, a territory it claims based on the 1816 Sugauli Treaty. India dismissed these claims as unjustified and historically unfounded, stating Lipulekh has been a long-standing yatra route. This dispute marks a potential test for bilateral ties as both nations prepare for diplomatic discussions.

title: US goes ahead with radar upgrade for Pakistan’s F-16 fleet, move a worry for India
content: The US is proceeding with a $488 million radar upgrade for Pakistan's F-16 fighter jets, a move expected to heighten Indian security concerns. This program, contracted to Northrop Grumman, will enhance Pakistan's air capabilities amidst regional tensions. India's own fighter squadron numbers are reportedly below the required strength for a two-front conflict, making this development particularly noteworthy.

title: Bengal SIR a ‘threat’ to our jobs: Professionals, government staff move court
content: Livelihoods are at stake as 27 lakh voters in Bengal are stripped of voting rights due to electoral roll deletions during SIR. Professionals, including a PSU officer, teacher, doctor, and insurance agent, have moved the Calcutta High Court, fearing their citizenship will be questioned, potentially leading to job termination and licence cancellation. Their appeals for urgent hearings were denied.

title: 'I don't know': Kapil Dev declines to comment on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Former Indian all-rounder Kapil Dev declined to comment on teenage IPL star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, saying experts like Sunil Gavaskar and Ravi Shastri are better suited to judge him. The 15-year-old Rajasthan Royals opener has scored 404 runs in IPL 2026, including a 36-ball century, earning widespread praise and India call-up discussions.

title: I am one of Brij Bhushan's six victims, reveals wrestler Vinesh Phogat
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat revealed she is one of six accusers of former WFI chief Brij Bhushan Singh. Her return to competition in his stronghold, Gonda, raises concerns about fairness and potential bias in officiating. Phogat, now a legislator, demands transparency, holding the government accountable for any incidents. The WFI president assures safety and denies bias.

title: Assembly poll results 2026: A defining test for BJP, Cong & regional satraps
content: As vote counting looms for five key states, the 2026 assembly elections represent a critical juncture for national parties and regional strongholds. The BJP seeks geographic expansion, while the Congress fights for relevance. Regional forces face mounting pressure, making the outcome a test of India's political trajectory.

title: Assembly elections 2026: From East to South, key battlegrounds to watch out for
content: After the counting begins and trends trickle in, these seats offer early clues to momentum, alliance cohesion and voter sentiment that can ripple across regions. From prestige contests involving chief ministers to urban battlegrounds testing new entrants, the outcomes in these constituencies will help explain whether incumbency holds or fractures. They also reflect how local issues such as welfare delivery, identity, urban infrastructure and leadership credibility interact with wider political narratives.

title: Admiral Tripathi meets Myanmar naval leadership, discusses steps to boost cooperation
content: Indian Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi concluded a visit to Myanmar, reinforcing defence ties and navy-to-navy cooperation. Discussions focused on enhancing training exchanges and operational engagements, including the handover of Indian-assisted maritime security equipment. This collaboration underscores India's strategic importance of Myanmar under its 'Neighbourhood First' policy.

title: Sai's 57, pacers help Gujarat Titans pull off tense win over Punjab Kings
content: Gujarat Titans edged past Punjab Kings by four wickets in a thrilling IPL clash. Sai Sudharsan anchored the chase with 57, while Washington Sundar stayed unbeaten on 40 to seal victory in the final over. Earlier, debutant Suryansh Shedge scored a fighting 57 for Punjab after early damage caused by Mohammed Siraj, Jason Holder and Kagiso Rabada.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for Monday
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 4, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election results 2026 LIVE: Counting of votes begins; all eyes on Vijay's TVK as DMK seeks second consecutive term
content: Majority of exit poll projections gave an advantage to DMK+. However, Axis My India gave a massive 98-120 seats to TVK

title: West Bengal election results LIVE: Vote counting under way for 293 seats as early trends roll in
content: The Election Commission of India has assured all political parties of a triple-layer security at all counting centres with specialised patrols in vulnerable zones; only authorised personnel with QR-coded IDs are permitted entry in the counting venue

title: Maharashtra bypoll results: Counting of votes underway for Baramati, Rahuri Assembly seats
content: The Baramati seat in Pune district fell vacant following the death of then Deputy CM and Nationalist Congress Party president Ajit Pawar in a plane crash

title: Assam election results LIVE: Counting of votes begins as BJP eyes third term, Congress-led Opposition alliance seeks to wrest power
content: Both alliances are gung-ho about bagging at least 64 seats, the simple majority mark in the 126-member Assembly

title: Kerala Election Results 2026 LIVE: Initial trends show Congress-led UDF leading as vote counting progresses
content: Kerala has 140 counting centres across 43 locations; Tight security arrangements have been made across Kerala

title: Counting of votes for Bagalkot, Davanagere South bypolls underway amid tight security
content: According to the Election Commission, the voter turnout in Bagalkot was 68.74%, and it was 68.43% in Davanagere South

title: Assembly elections 2026 results: Counting of votes begins across five States
content: The results will decide the next governments in Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and West Bengal

title: Puducherry Assembly election results 2026 LIVE: Counting begins in Puducherry as AINRC-led NDA eyes second term
content: Six counting centres have been set up across the Union Territory; Chief Electoral Officer says elaborate arrangements have been made for counting of votes

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Security tightened in sensitive areas ahead of poll verdict
content: Votes for eight constituencies will be counted in UC College, Aluva, and MA College of Arts, Kothamangalam, both within the rural limits

title: Counting of votes to be held at three centres in Ernakulam
content: Strong rooms will be opened at 6:30 a.m. in the presence of candidates or their authorised representatives, and counting will begin at 8 a.m. Postal ballots will be taken up first, followed by votes recorded in electronic voting machines

title: Counting begins for Umreth Assembly bypoll in Gujarat
content: Nearly 700 officials and employees, including 14 counting supervisors, 14 assistants and 14 micro supervisors, have been appointed for the counting of votes, the District Election Officer said

title: PM Modi, External Affairs Minister on major diplomatic outreach in May
content: The travels follow the tough message to Indian envoys on building the country’s image at the Heads of Mission conference

title: Koridang bypoll results: Counting of votes begins in Nagaland
content: The bypoll, necessitated by the demise of sitting BJP MLA Imkong L. Imchen in November last year, witnessed a strong voter turnout of 82.21% on April 9

title: Assembly elections 2026 updates: All set for counting, ECI promises transparency
content: The Chief Electoral Officer said 53,984 postal ballots had been issued to service voters, of which 20,028 had been received as of May 1.

title: Massive fire at scrap yard in Hyderabad’s Bholakpur triggers firefighting, late night evacuation
content: No human casualties have been reported. However, a dog domesticated within the warehouse died in the fire.

title: Dharmanagar bypoll results: Counting of votes begins in Tripura amid tight security
content: The by-election was necessitated following the death of the Speaker of the Tripura Assembly, Biswa Bandhu Sen, in December 2025

title: West Bengal waits with bated breath for May 4 Assembly election results
content: Counting will be held across 77 counting venues spread across 23 districts of the State; the Election Commission has deployed 432 counting observers (COs) to ensure transparent and smooth counting of votes on April 4.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Women make up over 80% of the additional voters
content: Of about 25.3 lakh more votes polled this time than in 2021, around 20.8 lakh came from women, the highest in the last four Assembly elections

title: All set for counting; ECI promises transparency
content: Heavy security as counting of votes polled for elections to Assemblies of Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal and Puducherry and  bye-elections in seven Assembly constituencies of five States to be taken up

title: Electoral debuts in Tamil Nadu’s past Assembly elections tell a mixed story of breakthroughs, missed chances
content: With the high profile debut of actor-politician C. Joseph Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam in the latest polls, expectations are high about whether the party can breach the Dravidian duopoly or merely dent its margins

title: India-Nepal border: Crossing a line
content: With Nepalese law enforcement agencies pushing for customs duty on Indian goods above ₹100, people living along the Indo-Nepal border are alarmed over a disruption to their lives. A report on their worry over what will happen next at the so-far open border that divides the two nations, but is also symbolic of centuries-old relationships that has diminished that separation in practice

title: Assam election verdict: Congress hopes to stop BJP run
content: The BJP-led NDA is confident of a hat-trick of electoral wins, while the Congress-led alliance seeks to shed the Opposition tag

title: Pune rape case: Father of murdered child demands death penalty for accused; opposition slams govt
content: The real accused is this Government, which is useless and incompetent; you lathi-charged the people who were carrying that child’s dead body, Raut said

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Kerala all set for counting day as ruling Left Democratic Front eyes third term, while Opposition UDF hopes for a comeback
content: Kerala had 883 candidates in the fray on April 9 when, as per the latest updates, 79.7% of the 2.71 crore-strong electorate exercised their franchise

title: Vijay’s Velankanni visit cancelled; visual of supporters chanting ‘TVK’ inside Basilica goes viral
content: Addressing reporters at the party office in Karattur near Gobichettipalayam, Sengottaiyan said TVK party president Vijay is to emerge as a decisive leader and that the 2026 verdict will be historic

title: Velankanni priest says Basilica was not informed about TVK chief Vijay’s plan to visit shrine
content: The priest clarified that the morning schedule of prayers was not disrupted. He said devotees had expressed concern and anguish over the incident, prompting the church administration to take up the matter with the district administration and the police.

title: India-Jamaica ties characterised by ‘continuity and change’: Jaishankar
content: In an interview with The Jamaican Gleaner, Mr. Jaishankar also said that both countries “must continue to work together to build a deeper and more resilient partnership that works for both our people and contributes to a more just world”

title: Tallest underwater human stack formed; Andaman sets two Guinness World Records in 2 days
content: Fourteen people, including Lieutenant-Governor D.K. Joshi, took part in forming the 22.3-metre-long stack, and they stayed under the water for three minutes, officials said

title: Saini urges Punjab OBCs to back BJP in Assembly polls
content: Addressing an OBC Sammelan in Rupnagar, Haryana CM Nayab Saini said the BJP ensures equal representation

title: Independent journalism punished, compliance rewarded under current regime, says Kharge
content: Sections of the media have been reduced to echoing the ruling establishment, while those who persist in asking questions are ‘targeted relentlessly’, says the Congress president while calling for deep introspection by all stakeholders on the occasion of World Press Freedom Day

title: Prohibitory orders imposed in Doda as AAP MLA returns home district after 8 months in jail
content: The lone AAP legislator in the Union Territory was arrested on September 8, 2025, following his ‘inappropriate remarks’ against then-Deputy Commissioner Harvinder Singh over the shifting of a health centre at Doda’s Kencha, and was booked under the Public Safety Act that allows preventive detention up to two years

title: BC communities hopeful of political reservations in local body elections
content: BC Dedicated Commission Chairman Rajiv Ranjan Mishra, who has been appointed to ensure political reservations for all communities in accordance to their population, has visited three North Andhra districts

title: India rejects Nepal’s objection to Kailash Manasarovar Yatra via Lipulekh Pass
content: India remains open to a “constructive interaction” with Nepal on all issues in the bilateral relationship, including on boundary issues through “dialogue and diplomacy”, the Ministry of External Affairs said

title: High cost of five kg FTL cylinders keeps new consumers away
content: Consumers say a new five kg FTL connection costs ₹1,760, which is very high, and the cylinder will not last for 41 days unlike the regular cylinder

title: ‘Kashmir will never be Pakistani and we will never become Pakistan’, says  Farooq Abdullah
content: “I pray that I will not die till I see the revival of Gandhi’s philosophy again”, says National Conference president

title: BJP closing seminaries with the help of J&K government: Mehbooba Mufti
content: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president says Omar Abdullah government remained silent as Siraj-ul-Uloom in Shopian in south Kashmir was closed

title: Tamil Nadu | UPSC to shortlist DGP candidates on May 11
content: The new DGP/HoPF will have a minimum tenure of two years irrespective of date of superannuation, as per Supreme Court guidelines in the Prakash Singh case

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: LDF, UDF keeping their fingers crossed in Kozhikode
content: Once an LDF stronghold, the district may not witness a repeat performance, while UDF remains confident despite its lacklustre post-2001 performance; NDA expects to improve its vote share

title: Meeting takes stock of monsoon preparedness in Kozhikode district
content: Measures will be taken to ensure water sanitation, the safety of school buildings and the cleanliness of wells and drinking water tanks in schools

title: Ahead of Misri’s expected Nepal visit, Kathmandu expresses concern to India, China on Kailash yatra over Lipulekh
content: Nepal reiterates territorial claim to Lipulekh Pass, flags concerns over Kailash Manasarovar Yatra; India says the border pass has been long standing route for the yatra since 1954; it is unclear whether the Foreign Secretary will get to meet Nepal PM during the visit originally slated for May 11-12

title: Karnataka leadership talks in Delhi unlikely in next few weeks
content: Just as a group of Congress legislators aspiring to become Ministers are planning to reach Delhi next week to press for Cabinet expansion, the leadership issue in the Congress is unlikely to be settled soon

title: Results of bypolls to two Assembly seats in Karnataka on May 4
content: Amid rumblings in the Muslim voter base, the Congress government — just weeks short of turning three years —  is hoping to retain both Davangere South and Bagalkot constituencies, even as the BJP is confident of winning both

title: J&K L-G galvanises massive public support against drug abuse, says neighbouring country using it for terrorism, radicalisation
content: On Mr. Sinha’s invitation, the Speaker of the House, Cabinet ministers, legislators, heads of regional parties, top security officials, religious leaders, educational institutes and administrators of hospitals attended a two-km-long rally

title: Jabalpur cruise tragedy death toll reaches 13; bodies of last two victims recovered
content: 28 people were rescued after a boat operated by M.P. tourism board capsized during a storm on April 30; high-level committee to probe the causes of the tragedy

title: Kerala Election Results 2026 Live Updates: UDF Leads In 20 Seats, LDF 8, BJP 4 In Early Trends
content: Kerala Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: The outcome is significant for all three alliances as they look to strengthen their presence in the state's changing political landscape.

content: Stock Market, Share Market, Sensex, Nifty Live Updates: Markets are expected to react to the results of assembly elections in four states and one union territory, even as exit polls from key state elections point to a largely stable political landsca

content: Tamil Nadu Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: While most exit polls have predicted DMK's second straight win, one suggested a huge wave of the "Vijay effect" in the state this time.

title: Puducherry Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: Early Trends Show NDA Advantage In Puducherry, AINRC In Lead
content: Puducherry Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: A record voter turnout of 89.87% was reported, marking the highest participation ever in the Union Territory since its merger with India in 1964.

title: Live Updates: BJP Widens Gap With Trinamool In Bengal, DMK Ahead In Tamil Nadu, Show Early Leads
content: Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: Exit polls predict a close competition between Trinamool Congress and BJP in West Bengal

title: "Power Cuts, CCTVs Turned Off": Mamata Banerjee's Big Charge Before Counting
content: Mamata Banerjee's allegations came hours before the counting of votes for West Bengal assembly elections was scheduled to begin.

title: Pinarayi Vijayan: The Leader Who Remade Kerala's Left
content: After he became Kerala's 12th chief minister in May 2016, Pinarayi Vijayan faced some of the hardest challenges of his political journey.

title: From Shadow To Spotlight: Being Abhishek Banerjee In Bengal Politics
content: Abhishek Banerjee entered the political turf in 2011, the same year that saw a historic shift in Bengal's power dynamics.

title: NTR, MGR, Jayalalithaa, Rajinikanth, Pawan Kalyan - Vijay, Respectfully, Is None Of Them
content: Vijay is unlike anything Tamil Nadu has ever see. Comparing him to other star-turned-politicians is tempting, but unfair.

title: Counting Of Votes For Bypolls In 7 Seats Across 5 States Today
content: Election results: Votes in these seats were cast on April 9 and April 23, along with the first and second phases of the assembly elections.

title: Suvendu Adhikari, The Student Who One-Upped The Master, And Is Trying Again
content: Adhikari joined Banerjee's Trinamool Congress in 1998 and entered the Bengal assembly as an MLA from the Kanthi Dakshin constituency in 2006.

title: Votes Are Being Counted Only On 293 Seats In Bengal Today. Here's Why
content: Amid reported incidents of violence and EVM tampering, complaints demanded repolls in 77 booths, 32 of which were in Falta.

title: Actor Vijay And His Search For A Big Political Role In Tamil Nadu
content: For Vijay, the electoral fate of cinema icons Sivaji Ganesan, Kamal Hassan, Vijaykanth, and Khushbu would serve as a cautionary tale.

title: Will N Rangaswamy Buck Puducherry Trend And Return To Power Again? 10 Points
content: Votes will be counted for 30 seats in Puducherry today, with Chief Minister N Rangaswamy seeking to break the Union Territory's trend and secure a second straight term.

title: Rain Lashes Several Parts Of Delhi, Brings Respite From Heat
content: The fresh spell of rain comes after similar showers were recorded in the city on Saturday, indicating changing weather conditions over the past few days.

title: Any US "Interference" In Hormuz Would Be Ceasefire Violation, Warns Iran
content: A senior Iranian official warned on Monday that Tehran would consider any US attempt to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz a breach of the ceasefire.
